settings.title: Monster Piercing | Segment Clickers
settings.text:
Step into effortless style with our Segment Clickers Collection, one of our most popular and loved jewellery ranges. With over 100 unique designs and multiple sizes available, there’s a perfect clicker for every piercing and personal style.

Crafted with precision and comfort in mind, each piece features a secure and easy-to-use clicker clasp, ensuring a smooth fit and reliable hold. Available in a wide range of premium materials including titanium, gold, surgical steel, and silver, our segment clickers are designed for durability, everyday wear, and modern elegance.

Whether you prefer minimalist hoops, gem-studded sparkle, or bold statement designs, this collection offers endless ways to express your individuality. Ideal for ear, nose, and septum piercings, our segment clickers combine function, comfort, and timeless fashion in every detail.

settings.question: Are segment clickers safe for new piercings?
settings.answer: Yes – titanium and solid gold segment clickers are safe for most healed and new piercings. For fresh piercings, we recommend using implant-grade titanium initially and switching to gold or steel once healing is complete.

settings.question: Can I use a segment clicker in any piercing?
settings.answer: Segment clickers are versatile and can be used in a range of piercings, including septum, helix, daith, conch, tragus, lobe, and nostril. Always check the gauge and diameter of your piercing to ensure a proper fit.

settings.question: What materials are your segment clickers made from?
settings.answer: We offer segment clickers in implant-grade titanium, solid 14K gold, 925 sterling silver, and 316L surgical steel. Each piece is polished to a mirror finish for smooth insertion, comfort, and long-lasting shine.

settings.question: How do I open and close a segment clicker?
settings.answer: Simply locate the small hinge or seam on the ring. Gently pull the segment open until it clicks free, insert it into your piercing, and push it back until you hear or feel a firm “click.” Always handle gently to maintain the shape of the ring.
